_comment: Tidal Access Model Configuration. Tidal curve parameters used by access window calculator. Harmonic constants and secondary port offsets are documented here for reference but are currently implemented in harmonic.py and secondary_port.py respectively.

secondary_port_offset._comment: Portsmouth to Langstone Harbour corrections. REFERENCE ONLY — these values are not read by any code at runtime. The operative constants are hardcoded in secondary_port.py (apply_offset) and khm_parser.py (parse_khm_paste). Any change to these values must also be made in those two files. Original validation April 2026 against UKHO half-hourly data for both ports observed HW height delta 0.0-0.1m. The 16-day calibration corpus subsequently showed that propagating a 0.05m HW height bump through curve interpolation introduced a +0.05m production-path mean bias with no offsetting RMS improvement; the height offset has been removed (set to 0). LW heights remain effectively identical between ports.

tidal_curve._comment: Parameters for Langstone Harbour asymmetric tidal curve used to interpolate heights between HW/LW events. Applied to the EBB phase only - the flood uses pure cosine interpolation. Read at runtime by access_calc._get_curve_params().
tidal_curve._unused: flood_duration_fraction and ebb_duration_fraction are retained below for documentation only. They are not applied in access_calc._curve_interpolate() — the actual HW/LW event timestamps already encode the asymmetric flood/ebb duration, so applying a further fraction correction would double-count it.
tidal_curve._notes: Recalibrated 29 April 2026 against a 16-day half-hourly UKHO corpus (14-22 April + 29 April-5 May 2026, 768 samples). Final values 70min / 96% selected by parameter sweep over 28 combinations (durations 60-100 min, fractions 0.94-0.97); see scripts/sweep_ebb_params.py. Result: ebb mean residual essentially zero (+0.002m), ebb RMS reduced from 0.278m to 0.139m (50% improvement), overall RMS reduced from 0.285m to 0.239m (16% improvement). The flood remains pure cosine; an attempt to apply the same stand to the flood phase was rolled back because it more than doubled flood RMS error - the visible flatness near HW in the flood is largely accounted for by the cosine's own slowdown near its peak; explicit stand on top double-counts that flatness and compresses the mid-flood cosine to be unrealistically steep. The flood retains a residual ~+0.13m mid-cycle bias that a different curve shape (not cosine) would be needed to address.

admiralty_convention_offset._comment: Post-processing time shifts applied to harmonic predict_events output to align mathematical peaks with Admiralty published HW/LW convention. REFERENCE ONLY — these values are not read by any code at runtime. The operative constants are HW_ADMIRALTY_OFFSET_MINUTES and LW_ADMIRALTY_OFFSET_MINUTES in harmonic.py. Any change must also be made there. Derived April 2026 from 710 Admiralty Portsmouth reference points (355 HW + 355 LW) across 6 months between Jul 2026 and Dec 2027. The mathematical peak of the harmonic curve falls systematically later than the Admiralty published time. These offsets do not affect predict_height_at_time() - heights throughout the tidal curve remain correct.

harmonic_reference._comment: Harmonic constants are implemented in app/harmonic.py. Documented here for reference only. 19 constituents calibrated April 2026 against 388 Admiralty reference points (91 HW/LW + 7 spring HW + 288 half-hourly) spanning May-December 2026 plus April 2026 half-hourly data. Validated April 2026 against a further 710 Admiralty points across 6 months between Jul 2026 and Dec 2027 (see admiralty_convention_offset).
harmonic_reference.accuracy: Post Admiralty offset correction: HW/LW timing bias near zero (HW +0.2min, LW -0.1min) with stdev 15min HW / 19min LW. Height bias near zero (HW +0.02m, LW +0.05m) with stdev 0.13m HW / 0.19m LW. Based on 710-point validation across Jul 2026 - Dec 2027.
